methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $177M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Bright Machines
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Carbon Robotics, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Paul Mikesell, has raised $177M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public company operating in robotics and manufacturing, including peers at adjacent stages: Exotec (Series D, $2.0B), Bedrock Robotics (Series B, $1.8B), Dexterity (Series B, $1.6B), Hadrian (Series C, $1.6B), and Nimble Robotics (Series C, $1.0B).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $301M on the $177M capital raised proxy. The peer-median method references five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies with a median valuation of $1.6B and an observed range of $1.0B to $2.0B, directly implying $1.6B.

The triangulated analysis produces a range from $256M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $975M. This wide spread reflects material uncertainty regarding Carbon Robotics' relative operational performance versus peers. Key drivers of variance include unit economics, revenue growth trajectory, and competitive positioning within manufacturing automation. The peer-median method suggests meaningfully higher valuations than stage-progression alone, warranting careful examination of whether Carbon Robotics' traction warrants premium positioning or a discount within the peer set.
